final_prompt.prompt:
Create an editorial photo of a traditional morning prayer ceremony on the Varanasi ghats along the Ganges River in India.

Style/medium: documentary street photography, humanistic photojournalism, 35mm film aesthetic with rich saturated colors, reminiscent of Steve McCurry's India work
Composition: golden ratio framing, shallow depth of field, foreground with blurred flower petals, middle ground centered on the priest, background showing the silhouette of the opposite riverbank, wide-angle establishing shot
Subject: elderly Hindu priest in orange saffron robes holding a brass oil lamp, surrounded by devotees performing rituals, some bathing in the river, others with hands clasped in prayer, some floating oil lamps on the water
Scene/backdrop: Varanasi ghats at dawn, hundreds of Hindu devotees gathered on stone steps descending to the Ganges, calm river surface reflecting countless small oil lamps with flowers floating
Lighting/mood: golden hour sunrise light rising from the horizon, warm golden tones washing over the entire scene, incense smoke diffusing the light, reverent and spiritual atmosphere
Color palette: warm golden sunrise as dominant tone, rich saffron orange from priest robes, brass and copper metallics, soft pink and white flower accents, deep blue-green river water
Materials/textures: weathered stone steps, flowing water with gentle ripples, brass oil lamp patina, silk and cotton fabric of robes, flower petals, film grain texture
Avoid: oversaturated HDR look, plastic skin texture, deformed hands, watermark, 8K stuffer aesthetic, oversharpening
